perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with an older man enjoying a spring roll, followed by a series of close-up shots showing the assembly of the roll: spreading peanut powder, adding noodles, shredded egg, meat, cabbage, and carrots on a thin wrapper. (2) The scene shifts to a bright modern kitchen where the main female creator, wearing a black 'HAHABABY' t-shirt, introduces the ingredients laid out on the counter. (3) Chronological cooking sequences show her stir-frying cabbage, boiling bamboo shoots and carrots in water, pan-frying seasoned pork belly until golden brown, and shredding cooked chicken by hand while wearing gloves. (4) A young boy in a yellow shirt enters the frame, and the woman helps him roll his own spring roll; he then takes a large bite with a happy expression. (5) The video concludes with the woman and a male friend in a blue sweater discussing the tradition of adding noodles to spring rolls, pointing at the ingredients and laughing while text overlays ask the audience for their opinion.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, medium-tempo acoustic-pop background music starts immediately. (2) A female voice begins speaking in Mandarin with an enthusiastic and friendly tone, introducing the Ching Ming festival tradition. (3) Crisp foley sounds are prominent: the 'hm-mm' of satisfaction while eating, the sizzling of meat in a pan, the bubbling of boiling water, and the rustling of plastic gloves. (4) Occasional interjections from a male voice (fatherly tone) and the sound of a child chewing and responding 'mm-hmm' to a question. (5) The audio ends with a lively multi-person conversation and laughter as they discuss regional food differences.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: '清明' (Ching Ming) title card with traditional motifs. (2) Ingredient labels: '高麗菜' (Cabbage), '竹筍' (Bamboo shoots), '紅蘿蔔' (Carrots), '鹹豬肉' (Salty pork), '雞肉' (Chicken), '麵' (Noodles), '蛋' (Egg). (3) Decorative/Hook Text: '完成啦 YUMMY' (Finished, Yummy), '全家大小一起DIY' (Whole family DIY together), '潤餅南北之戰' (Spring roll North-South battle), '有麵派? 無麵派?' (Team Noodles? Team No Noodles?), '底下留言' (Comment below).

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro Hook -> Ingredient Showcase -> Cooking Process -> Family DIY -> Social Debate/CTA

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator, 1 child, 1 older male at start, 1 male in blue sweater, 1 female assistant in background.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young woman with dark hair tied back, wearing a black oversized t-shirt with the 'HAHABABY' logo.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A clean, modern white kitchen with stainless steel appliances, a black induction stove, and organized shelving.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a festive tutorial and lifestyle vlog aimed at celebrating the Ching Ming festival. It demonstrates a family's unique spring roll recipe while emphasizing family bonding through DIY cooking. It also utilizes a 'North-South' cultural debate regarding the inclusion of noodles to drive audience engagement and comments.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The creator is wearing clothing from her own brand 'HAHABABY' throughout the video, and specific kitchen products are visible during use.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Usage of the brand 'HAHABABY' and the specific family-oriented 'DIY' culinary style.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video successfully blends traditional cultural practices with modern short-video engagement tactics like 'this or that' debates.
